completed_work: Updated ClaudeProvider to accept max_tokens and pass self._max_tokens to Anthropic messages.create.
Added tests for default 4096 max_tokens and custom 2048 max_tokens.
Verified full pytest suite at commit time: 225 passed.
key_decisions: Use a code-level CLAUDE_MAX_TOKENS default rather than a secret or environment-dependent setting.
Keep ClaudeProvider backwards compatible by defaulting max_tokens to CLAUDE_MAX_TOKENS.
next_actions: Use the larger Claude cap for synthesis tasks and revisit provider output configuration only if truncation recurs.
risks: 4096 is still a finite provider output cap; very long syntheses may require future tuning.
OpenAI provider still has no explicit analogous output cap in the local wrapper.
summary_text: AI-Lab raised Claude provider output capacity by replacing hard-coded max_tokens=1024 with configurable CLAUDE_MAX_TOKENS=4096. ClaudeProvider now accepts an optional max_tokens constructor argument and passes self._max_tokens to Anthropic messages.create. Tests cover the default 4096 cap and a custom 2048 cap. This addressed truncated Claude synthesis artifacts while keeping provider configuration code-level and non-secret. Full suite passed with 225 tests at commit time.
